question_text: Let {Xn}n≥0 be a time-homogeneous discrete time Markov chain with either finite or countable state space S. Then which one of the following statements is true?

options.A: There is at least one recurrent state
options.B: If there is an absorbing state, then there exists at least one stationary distribution
options.C: If all the states are positive recurrent, then there exists a unique stationary distribution
options.D: If {Xn}n≥0 is irreducible, S = {1, 2} and [π1 π2] is a stationary distribution, then limn→∞P(Xn = i | X0 = i) = πi for i = 1, 2

solution:
- Option (A) is true because a time-homogeneous Markov chain always has at least one
recurrent state.
- Option (B) is false because if there is an absorbing state, it does not necessarily guarantee
the existence of a stationary distribution.
- Option (C) is true because if all states are positive recurrent, a unique stationary distribution
exists.
- Option (D) is true by the fundamental limit theorem of Markov chains, which states that for
an irreducible Markov chain with a stationary distribution, limn→∞P(Xn = i | X0 = i) = πi.
Final Answer:
(D) If {Xn}n≥0 is irreducible, S = {1, 2} and [π1 π2] is a stationary distribution, then lim

Quick Tip
For irreducible Markov chains with a stationary distribution, the chain will converge to
the stationary distribution as n →∞.
